commands.review_summary:
The branch changes shared reply payload filtering so text-only finals after successful block streaming are preserved unless the block reply pipeline reports that exact payload was already sent, and it updates focused reply tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +30. Total +36 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source: current main drops a text-only final after a successful non-aborted block stream without checking whether that exact final payload was sent, and the existing focused test codifies the drop.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is real runtime proof or maintainer acceptance of that proof gap, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper can safely generate on the branch.
